title: Meta Ray-Bans may have exposed your most intimate moments: Here's what every owner must do immediately
snippet: Meta is facing scrutiny for its privacy policies regarding user data collection through devices like Ray-Ban Smart Glasses. Cybersecurity experts highlight loopholes that compromise data safety, urging users to disable unnecessary sharing and be vigilant about their recordings.

title: Nothing Phone 4a with Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, Glyph Bar launched in India: Price, specs and more
snippet: Nothing has launched the Phone (4a), a mid-range smartphone featuring a 50MP periscope telephoto lens and a 6.78-inch AMOLED display. The phone offers a Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, 12GB RAM, and a 5,400mAh battery, available in four colors via Flipkart.

title: Apple launches Mac Neo at  ₹69,900: Top features, specs and how it compares to MacBook Air
snippet: Apple's MacBook Neo is an entry-level laptop aimed at students, featuring a 13-inch Liquid Retina display, A18 Pro processor, and macOS Tahoe. It offers 16 hours of battery life, a 1080p FaceTime HD camera, and weighs 2.7 pounds, making it a competitive option against the MacBook Air and Pro.

title: MacBook price drop in 2026: Why buying the M3 and M4 models is a smart choice right now
snippet: Maximize your 2026 savings with our guide to the MacBook price crash. Learn why the M3 and M4 are now the smartest buys for your budget and how to avoid the new model price trap.

title: Nothing Phone 4a Launch Highlights: Nothing Phone 4a, Phone 4a Pro launched in India
snippet: Nothing Phone 4a Launch Highlights: Nothing has launched the Phone 4a, Phone 4a Pro in India alongside the new Headphone (a).

title: Realme Narzo Power 5G with Dimensity 7400 SoC, 10,001 mAh battery launched in India: Price, specs and more
snippet: Realme launched the Narzo Power 5G in India featuring a 10,001 mAh battery, Dimensity 7400 processor, and priced from  ₹27,999. First sale offers include discounts and a free 4-year battery warranty. The smartphone features a 6.8-inch AMOLED display and robust water resistance ratings.

title: Apple launches its lowest priced MacBook. Why it’s such a big deal.
snippet: The MacBook Neo is less expensive than the iPhone 17.

title: Apple unveils MacBook Neo, brings premium features to a lower price segment: Check India price, launch details
snippet: Apple introduced the MacBook Neo on 4 March, priced from $599, targeting budget-conscious consumers. Powered by the A18 Pro chip, it features a 13-inch Liquid Retina display and offers up to 16 hours of battery life.&nbsp;

title: Apple unveils $599 MacBook Neo with iPhone A18 Pro chip in budget laptop push
snippet: The MacBook has a 13.0-inch screen, making it one of the company’s smallest laptops to date. The MacBook Air, in contrast, offers a 13.6-inch display. Another twist: The Neo runs on an iPhone A18 Pro chip, marking the first time Apple is using a smartphone processor in a Mac.

title: Oppo confirms global rollout for Find X9 Ultra, teases advanced Hasselblad camera system: here's what to expect
snippet: Oppo has teased the Find X9 Ultra. The phone is set to debut globally. While India availability is unconfirmed, leaks indicate a strong camera focus with a 200MP lens. The phone could come wtih support for 100W fast charging

US mulls new rules for AI chip exports, including requiring US investments by foreign firms
snippet: United States officials are considering new rules to control exports of AI chips, possibly requiring foreign nations to invest in U.S. data centres or provide security guarantees. The proposal could affect sales by Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices, marking a shift from the approach under Joe Biden.

title: Amazon down for thousands of users in US: Downdetector
snippet: Amazon experienced a website outage affecting thousands of users in the United States. Reports on Downdetector showed problems with logging in, browsing and checkout. Minor disruptions also appeared on Prime Video and Amazon Web Services, while the exact cause of the issue remained unclear.

title: Oracle layoffs planned as data centre costs surge: Bloomberg
snippet: Oracle plans thousands of job cuts as it struggles with cash pressure from a huge AI data-centre expansion. The cloud company is investing heavily to support deals with OpenAI, xAI and Meta. Investors worry about rising debt, while hiring slows and some roles may shrink due to AI.

title: Anthropic deemed supply chain risk 'effective immediately': Pentagon
snippet: The Pentagon has labelled Anthropic a supply-chain risk after its CEO Dario Amodei refused military use that could enable surveillance or autonomous weapons. Some contractors may stop using Claude. Critics call the action an overreach, while downloads surge and rivalry with OpenAI intensifies.

title: Banned RMG games thrive in India; green shoots in tech hiring

title: Infosys tells senior staff to attend office four days a week
snippet: This is the latest in a series of steps the software major has taken to increase employee footfall in the office. The development comes amid peers also tightening return-to-office norms.
